name: luly-v0-6-0
description: Author Luly flows end-to-end from a single natural-language prompt. v0.6.0 (minor ‚Äî flow courses in academies): an academy hub can now contain a 'flow course' ‚Äî a `flowType:'simple'` card that opens a SHORT LINEAR FLOW (no course-details landing, no lessons; screens play in sequence and the top-right close returns to the hub), alongside full learning courses. This is the clean, app-native way to put a lead-gen / 'leave your details' mini-flow (a few screens ending in a form) into an academy ‚Äî previously the only option was a full learning course wrapper or a manual merge with hand-rewired controls. Authoring: /luly-plan emits an optional, repeatable `## Flow course ‚Äî <title>` block (with `description:` and `form: yes`); /luly-fill writes its screens under `## Flow K ¬∑ Screen N ‚Äî <title>`. The assembler builds each as a simple course ‚Üí single default-section wrapper lesson ‚Üí screens, appended to the hub; controls are baked per position ‚Äî course/lesson auto-nav, every screen's close ‚Üí hub, Back from screen 2 on, and the LAST screen has only Back (its form's submit is the terminal action), no finishLesson. No app change was needed (the renderer already skips the course-details view for simple courses ‚Äî ContentRenderer.tsx). Academy preset only; assemble errors if `## Flow course` is used elsewhere or its `## Flow K` screens are missing. v0.5.0 (minor ‚Äî new block model: sections + containers): regenerated to match luly-app's nodeId/parentNodeId refactor and the new block system. Screens now hold MULTIPLE blocks composed as a tree ‚Äî `container` (responsive row/column with per-child `flex`), `section` (full-bleed band), and `slider` (carousel) hold child blocks, nested in the screen's flat block list via `parentSlug` + `lexoRank` (exactly what the CMS editor mints and what import expects). The retired monolithic composites (`image-richtext` / `quiz-text` / `form-text`) are NO LONGER EMITTED ‚Äî the `media-text` / `quiz-text` / `form-text` preset sugar now expands to a real `container` with two flex children (3 / 4), matching the app's getSampleContentForFormat defaults. content.md gains a `+++` block delimiter plus `as:` / `parent:` / `flex:` / `slot:` for nesting; new leaf formats `animation` and `button`. The validator now checks parentSlug integrity + lexoRank uniqueness per screen, adds nodeId/parentNodeId to the audit-field denylist, and recognizes the new space/learning_path content types. The assembler's reslug is now parentSlug-aware so cloned stub courses keep their nesting. The bundled stub-course.json and the app's product-templates were regenerated into the new model, and the dead block-validation.ts was removed. Navigation/controls fixes: simple presets (campaign-simple / waitlist / interactive-proposal) now emit the hub + course + lesson auto-nav controls they were missing ‚Äî previously the course/lesson had no navigation action, so every simple flow failed validation and couldn't descend hub‚Üícourse‚Üílesson‚Üíscreen. And lesson-screen Next controls are now position-baked at generation time, so `finishLesson` lands ONLY on the actual LAST screen of a lesson (replacing the runtime `isLastScreen` guard whose old misfire could finish a lesson from its first screen); applied to authored AND stub-course screens. Controls are now driven by the real content screens (not the plan), so positions/keys stay correct even when fill splits a screen. v0.4.1 (patch): the course landing screen now has a 'Back' button (‚Üí parent = the academy hub) alongside 'Learn' ‚Äî previously a course only offered 'Learn' with no way back to the hub. Added to both authored courses (controls-presets courseControls) and template/stub courses (bundled fixture), mirroring the canonical academy template. Stub clones also get fresh control ids so repeated stubs don't share control identity. v0.4.0 (minor ‚Äî template courses): an academy can now carry multiple courses, where extra ones are content-less 'template' stubs (a real catalog card ‚Äî title + description ‚Äî backed by a default 3-lesson scaffold the user fills later in the CMS). Strictly OPT-IN: default behavior is one authored course; stubs are added only when the user asks for several/placeholder courses or clearly lists course topics. /luly-plan emits an optional `## Template courses` list (Title | description); omitting the `## Section N` blocks yields an onboarding-only academy with just stubs. The assembler builds each stub from a bundled, sanitized copy of the app's default-course template (Onboarding + Completion lessons removed; screen backgrounds + control color overrides stripped so the PRODUCT THEME applies to every screen ‚Äî no custom backgrounds), gives every node a fresh slug (controls use relative targets, no reference rewrite), and uses the flow's shared branded placeholder for card + icon. v0.3.3 (patch ‚Äî logos & image sizes): (1) Real-logo bug fix ‚Äî the assembler's loadInlineSvg required SVG markup to start with `<svg`, so real downloaded brand logos that lead with an `<?xml?>` prolog / `<!DOCTYPE>` / comments (Wikipedia, CDNs) were silently dropped ‚Äî no header logo, no hub logo. It now slices from the first `<svg`, so real logos inline correctly. (2) Logo priority ‚Äî header prefers the icon-only mark (short), hub prefers the lockup-with-name (long); PNG/JPEG URLs accepted as fallback. (3) Logos are real-or-asked, never fabricated ‚Äî when no real logo/icon is found after the full research ladder, the pipeline asks the user (abstract stand-in vs neutral placeholder) instead of drawing a fake; dead `hub-logo.svg` generation removed. (4) Card covers are abstract ‚Äî no text, no wordmark, no literal product; calm brand-color shapes. (5) Image sizes aligned to the app: media placeholder is SQUARE 800√ó800 (app media block defaults to 1:1), card cover is a wide ~10:3 banner (1600√ó480) matching the app default card.png (503√ó151, cover-cropped fixed-height), icon stays 1:1. v0.3.2 (patch): fix a v0.3.1 regression ‚Äî the post-assembly validator still REQUIRED `body.theme.layout`, but v0.3.1 made the assembler omit `layout` when a flow has no overrides (the common case), so every default flow failed validation with "body.theme.layout missing" and had to be hand-patched. The validator now treats `layout` as optional (validates its shape only when present), matching the assembler and the app renderer (which falls back to the 1480px desktop default when layout is absent). v0.3.1 (minor ‚Äî schema accuracy, mobile/desktop, color application): (1) Layout-token fix ‚Äî flow-level layout overrides use the renderer's suffixed keys `maxWidthDesktop` / `paddingDesktop` (+ `*Mobile`); the old bare `maxWidth` default was silently ignored and is dropped, so flows keep the app default desktop width (1480px) unless deliberately overridden. (2) Color application ‚Äî luly-style now documents where key tokens actually render (quiz answer cards: `onSurface` unselected, `primaryLight` selected, `successLight` correct, `failureLight` wrong; `secondary` dark callouts with `textOnPrimary` text) and adds mandatory contrast targets for those answer-card and dark-callout pairs. (3) Per-viewport awareness ‚Äî skills now reference the `*Mobile` style tokens and per-block margin model (`margin*` / `margin*Mobile`, defaults top 16 / bottom 0) as polishing levers for the future agentic-edit scope, explicitly NOT emitted during simple generation.
